rules: Understand what i need, if task is not clear ask me question if its clear no need
Implementation must be scalable and maintainable.
Maintain consistency with the existing architecture, folder structure, naming conventions, and coding patterns.
Do not introduce runtime errors.
Always work on the main branch.
Commit changes locally without pushing.
After a successful commit, remove the completed step from the plan.
Follow DRY principles.
Reuse existing components and patterns whenever possible.
Avoid overengineering. Build the simplest scalable solution that meets current requirements. Do not add abstractions, optimizations, or features without a clear need.

Wall junctions: adding or removing any junction type or join style must be trivial — classify junction kinds in one classifier and keep join styles in a registry (Record<JoinStyle, JoinResolver>); no junction switch-ladders in the renderer.

Wall junctions: any value an architect may need to change manually (join style, miter limit, end cap, thickness-mismatch alignment) must be a configurable setting in editor.store with a UI control — never hard-coded. Geometric-truth constants (SNAP_EPSILON, collinear tolerance) stay as module constants.
Reuse existing code first (computeTopology, wallAngles, wallGeometry, dimensionChains, wallLayers); create new files only when no existing one can host the concern.
Curved/arc walls are a separate shape variant and tool (a distinct option alongside the straight wall), not a flag bolted onto the straight wall.
No temporary, partial, or construction-incompatible solutions; every junction must be a geometrically correct solid suitable for takeoff, consistent with the construction purpose of the project.
Every junction step must update dimensions and dimension display in the same step (measurementReference faces, per-segment dimensions, and dimensionChains break-points), never as a later patch.
Wall height (WallShape.height) is carried in 2D now for future 3D: set it on creation, keep it editable in 2D, and preserve/propagate it through every junction and edit path — never drop it.
If current task related to juctions or dimentions, See .claude/skills/wall-junctions for the authoritative geometry/computation reference for all 20 junction types.

All user-facing terminology must be localized in Italian (it), Persian (fa), English (en), and German (de). Use professional construction, architecture, and building-design terminology rather than generic translations. Wall types, junctions, join styles, dimensions, layers, structural elements, editor controls, validation messages, and all future features must follow the existing i18n system. Translation keys must be scalable and maintainable. The implementer is responsible for choosing the appropriate industry-standard terminology in each language.
use /compact to reduce token usage on every step complete

Estimation evolution: the pure cost reducer (core/estimation/estimate.ts) math stays unchanged. Only the INPUT side grows — the takeoff/quantity-sourcing and catalog/authoring layers. Never fork or duplicate the cost engine.

A priced line is a neutral WorkItem. The existing wall + space (floor/ceiling) assembly path must become ONE producer of WorkItems, not a parallel system. Every new estimate line (demolition, fixed charge, fixtures, linear work) is a WorkItem feeding the same estimate() reducer.

Spaces and all geometry remain DERIVED runtime entities. Only assignments and manually-authored quantities are persisted in the document (mirroring spaceAssignments) — geometry is never duplicated or turned into an editable drawing object.

Quantities may be derived (from geometry), manual (user-entered count/length/area), or fixed (lump sum). The unit model (m2/m3/ml/each/kg) stays the single source of truth — do not introduce a parallel quantity-type system.
Reuse the existing recommendation engine (core/estimation/recommend.ts + components/estimation/AssemblyOptions.tsx) for any cost alternatives. Do not fork the ranking logic for tasks/trades.

Pricing rules must evolve additively and stay backward-compatible with saved rules: the global material|labor|total target keeps working as-is; any new scope (per-trade / per-element-type / per-work-item) is an optional extra field that defaults to the current global behaviour. Never migrate or break persisted rules/rates/questions in localStorage.

Operations on existing elements (demolition, floor removal, disposal) are first-class WorkItems. Do not abuse pricing rules or question flags to fake fixed charges, optional lines, or quantity-bearing items — flags stay for conditional modifiers only.
Missing prices/assemblies/quantities are always surfaced as warnings, never silently dropped (keep the buildSpaceItems warning pattern for every WorkItem source).

steps.title: Generic selection-action sheet (space + non-wall shapes)
steps.goal: Generalize the wall-only WallActions FAB+modal into a selected-element sheet that renders the right existing form, so rooms and non-wall shapes are actionable on mobile.
steps.rationale: A room is tap-selectable but SpacePropertiesForm is desktop-Inspector-only and WallActions opens only for walls — selecting a space/line/text/opening on mobile offers nothing beyond More -> Delete.
details: Switch the sheet on selection: WallPropertiesForm (wall/arc-wall), SpacePropertiesForm (space), a minimal delete + relevant props otherwise.
Reuse the existing forms; respect selection.store's mutual exclusion of shape vs space.
unlocks: Editing rooms + non-wall shapes on touch

steps.title: Grab-vs-pan disambiguation near targets
steps.goal: Give selection priority within an enlarged target band so a near-miss selects/moves rather than silently panning the canvas.
steps.rationale: In the merged select+pan tool a single finger landing just off a thin line pans with no hover to reveal the miss; touch-sized hit radii exist but the miss is silent.
details: Keep the touch hit radii; widen the 'treat as grab' band before falling through to pan in the select tool.
Tune only — do not remove the deliberate drag-empty-to-pan affordance.
unlocks: Fewer accidental pans on small targets

steps.title: Long-press / double-tap gestures (headroom)
steps.goal: Add long-press on a shape (contextual actions: delete / duplicate over existing store calls) and double-tap on empty space (zoom-to-fit).
steps.rationale: Tap/drag/pinch are the only gestures today; long-press and double-tap are unused headroom that match professional touch CAD. Additive — only after the precision/flow steps land.
details: Add a gesture layer in useStageEvents (timers + movement thresholds) that does NOT regress the existing tap/drag/pinch routing or the second-finger cancel.
Reuse existing store actions; no new geometry.
unlocks: Contextual actions + double-tap fit on touch

steps.title: Pointer-id input capture (palm/stray-touch robustness)
steps.goal: Route canvas gestures by a tracked pointer id instead of touches.length so a resting palm / stray second finger doesn't cancel an in-progress stroke.
steps.rationale: Current routing is by touch count; a stray touch cancels the active gesture (safe but lossy). Pointer-id capture is a core input refactor with regression risk — do it LAST, after the feature steps are stable.
details: Track the active pointerId in useStageEvents / useStageViewport; ignore non-primary pointers for the active tool gesture while still honouring two-finger pinch.
Must preserve desktop behaviour and the per-frame perf contract; verify no regression in pan / pinch / draw / transform.

steps.title: WorkItem primitive (keystone)
steps.goal: Introduce a neutral WorkItem at the takeoff↔estimate boundary so a cost line is no longer hard-bound to a material-layer assembly + geometry-derived measure.
steps.rationale: estimate() is already a general reducer over EstimateItem; the geometry/material coupling lives only in the takeoff and catalog. A neutral WorkItem is the single unlock for demolition, fixed charges, fixture counts, linear work and optional items — they all become producers of WorkItems instead of special cases.
details: Define WorkItem (core/estimation): { id, name, unit, quantitySource, rate-or-layers, category?, inclusion? }. Keep EstimateItem as the reducer input; a WorkItem maps to an EstimateItem.
Refactor the existing wall grouping + buildSpaceItems(floor/ceiling) to EMIT WorkItems that map to EstimateItems — one producer, no parallel pipeline.
estimate() and recommend() math stay byte-for-byte unchanged.
Keep the missing-assembly/missing-price warning behaviour for every WorkItem.

steps.title: Manual & fixed quantity sources
steps.goal: Allow a WorkItem quantity to be manual (user-entered count/length/area) or fixed (lump sum), not only geometry-derived.
steps.rationale: Most non-fitting quotation lines fail purely because the only quantity source today is drawn geometry (wall=1, space=1). A manual/fixed source closes the largest single gap.
details: quantitySource = 'derived' | 'manual' | 'fixed'.
Persist manual/fixed quantities in the document like spaceAssignments (per project and/or per room) — never duplicate geometry; keep inside the zundo temporal wrapper for undo/redo.
Fixed charge = a WorkItem with quantity 1 and a flat rate, rendered as its own line (NOT a pricing rule).
Reuse m2/m3/ml/each/kg; no new quantity-type system.
unlocks: Fixed-cost items (site setup, permits, scaffolding lump sum)
Standalone linear work (skirting, cornice, pipe runs)

steps.title: Line-item inclusion state (optional / excluded)
steps.goal: Give each WorkItem an inclusion state and teach the totaller to respect it.
steps.rationale: Real quotations carry priced-but-optional add-ons and zero-cost documented exclusions; today there is no inclusion concept and recommendations are replacements, not optional add-ons.
details: inclusion = 'included' | 'optional' | 'excluded'.
included → in base total; optional → priced + shown but out of total (presented as add-on); excluded → zero-cost informational line.
estimate() change is minimal and additive (selective summation); default 'included' keeps current behaviour.

steps.title: Operations on existing elements (demolition / removal / disposal)
steps.goal: Model WorkItems that act on existing/removed elements, and derive disposal quantity from them.
steps.rationale: Renovation (vs new-build) requires costing existing elements that the takeoff deliberately excludes; demolition/removal have no material build-up — they are tasks, and disposal is a quantity derived from them.
details: Lift the takeoff `existing` exclusion FOR TASKS only (not for new-build assemblies): a WorkItem may reference an existing wall/floor.
Add a derived-from-item quantity source so waste disposal (m³ debris / skip count) follows demolition volume.
Keep `existing` walls excluded from new-construction assembly takeoff (unchanged).

steps.title: Trades & phases grouping (presentation over existing data)
steps.goal: Group the estimate by SystemCategory (trade) and optionally by phase.
steps.rationale: Shapes already carry SystemCategory (architectural/structural/electrical/plumbing/hvac/…); once WorkItems carry a category, grouping is a presentation layer over data that already exists — lowest risk, do after items exist.
details: WorkItem carries a category (default by element/task type, mirroring categoryOf).
Group/subtotal by trade in the estimate panel + result; phase is purely organisational (no pricing effect).
Localize trade/phase labels (en/it/de/fa) via the existing i18n system.

steps.title: Scoped pricing rules (optional, later)
steps.goal: Let a pricing rule optionally target a trade / element type / work item, not only the global material|labor|total.
steps.rationale: Some surcharges/discounts apply to one trade or item (e.g. +10% on electrical, fixed access fee on demolition). The current global model is fine as a default; scoping is an additive enhancement.
details: Add an optional `scope` to PricingRule (default = global, current behaviour) — additive, backward-compatible with persisted rules.
estimate() applies a scoped rule against the matching items' subtotal instead of the global base; global rules unchanged.
No migration of saved rules/rates/questions; missing scope = global.
